steps.title: Identify the key finding
steps.content:
The researchers found that the expected density‑size relationship held for the medieval settlements.
This shows that, contrary to their hypothesis, the pattern did not differ from that of modern cities.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B captures this by saying there is a notable consistency between medieval and modern cities in the underlying social and economic interactions that shape urban development.
The other three either claim a limitation, a difference, or an uneven effect that the passage does not support.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
